last_item: install/upgrade validateUpgradeTarget (handlers_pgversion.go:858) — the sole guard stopping a destructive same-major/downgrade/unsupported-target major upgrade, gating BOTH the preflight (:240) and start (:324) endpoints, had ZERO tests (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED: added TestValidateUpgradeTarget (internal/server/handlers_pgversion_test.go), a table test driving the real pure function. A major upgrade runs pg_upgradecluster over the live datadir, so accepting a same-major (16->16), downgrade (17->16), or unsupported (16->99) target — or proceeding when the current major couldn't be read (current<=0) — is data-loss-class. Cases: accept 16->17 and 15->17; reject 17->16 + 16->16 (CodeValidation, msg 'newer'), 16->99 (CodeValidation, 'not a supported'), 0->17 + -1->17 (CodeInternal, 'current'). Each rejecting case pins a distinct code+message so no branch can borrow another's error. No bug found — the guard is correct; the test locks its contract against silent weakening. Mutation-proven: flipping target<=current->'<', current<=0->'<', and \!IsSupported(target)->false each reds the exact matching subtest (verified then reverted via git checkout). code-reviewer clean (no findings); test-skeptic enumerated every one-line mutation class (boundary flips, reorders, dropped branches, wrong code/message, swapped current/target) and found NO escaping mutation — the same-major 16->16 case sits exactly on the <= boundary a weaker test would omit. Gates green (gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 static build); web untouched; e2e not needed (pure unit).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
